market_summary: S&P 500 closed at 6,967.38 on April 14 (+2.21% from April 11 Friday close of ~6,817), with the index subsequently setting a new all-time record of 7,022.95 on April 15. The index is now 8%+ above its March trough, having fully erased Liberation Day losses. Nasdaq outperformed, advancing 1.59% on April 14 alone. Tech led broadly — software stocks +3%+ on the week; NVDA posted its 11th consecutive winning session, its longest streak on record. ASML Q1 2026 earnings (April 15) beat estimates (EUR 8.8B revenue vs EUR 8.5B consensus, net income EUR 2.8B vs EUR 2.5B expected) and guidance was raised to EUR 36-40B from prior EUR 34-39B. However the stock fell approximately 6% post-earnings due to tightening US export controls on China-bound equipment. AMZN surged +3.45% on April 14 on AWS AI momentum (AI run rate $15B, $200B 2026 capex commitment; CEO letter described as most impactful yet). Gold (GLD) at $445.09 on April 14, down approximately 12.5% from the January 29 all-time high of GLD $509.70, as the risk-on rally and Iran ceasefire continue to erode safe-haven demand.
macro_themes: 1. FED: Funds rate unchanged at 3.50-3.75%. Consensus for two cuts in full-year 2026 — timing constrained by tariff-driven inflation. Core PCE at 3.1% with tariff feedthrough projected to push headline toward 3.6% YoY by June. The Fed confirmed tariffs have prevented inflation from returning to 2% target. Powell term expires May 2026 — succession uncertainty is a live policy risk. 2. TARIFFS: 90-day pause on higher tariff rates (ex-China at 125%) continues. US average effective tariff rate remains in double digits. Business investment suppressed by policy uncertainty. Tariff-driven goods inflation accelerating into Q2 2026. IMF 2026 Article IV consultation warned of limited room for rate cuts given inflation persistence and upside commodity price risks. 3. GEOPOLITICS: Iran ceasefire struck April 7-9. Risk-off shock fully resolved — directly fuelled the April equity rally and S&P 500 to new ATH. Near-term Middle East risk premium largely removed. 4. CHINA TECH RESTRICTIONS: Actively tightening. ASML faces new export control headwinds (key driver of post-earnings -6% drop). NVIDIA H20 chips restricted since April 2025 with $5.5B inventory charge already taken. H200 situation complex — US partially eased with 25% surcharge but China reportedly blocking H200 imports. Broader semiconductor equipment sector under sustained China restriction pressure. 5. EQUITIES AT ATH: S&P 500 at record 7,022.95 (April 15). Driven by Q1 earnings beats across banks and tech, robust AI infrastructure capex cycle, and geopolitical risk resolution. Risk-on environment fully restored.
portfolio_health: Grade: C+ (75/100). NAV $4,320.67 — up $129.67 (+3.09%) from April 4 seed baseline of $4,191. Week return +2.24% vs S&P 500 +2.21% — essentially in line, with marginal +0.03pp outperformance. With the S&P 500 now at all-time highs, the portfolio's structural CSPX underweight is costing compounding returns daily. Critical failure persists for the FIFTH consecutive weekly report: the $250 CSPX buy first recommended April 11 remains entirely unexecuted. CSPX underweight: 25.49% of NAV vs 36% target = -10.5pp gap. Cash at 12.68% is structural drag. ASML fell approximately 6% post-earnings on April 15 (outside current data window at April 14 EOD) — actual ASML mark is closer to ~$248 vs $264 in XML. Total unrealized P&L: +$203.63 (+5.71% on cost basis $3,569.05). WHT structure: clean — only $3.33 withheld YTD. Commissions YTD: $67.38.

positions.rationale: S&P 500 at a new all-time high of 7,022.95 on April 15. CSPX at $746.64, +5.69% from cost $706.41. Position is 25.49% of NAV vs 36% target — a 10.5pp underweight. BUY $250 CSPX NOW. This is the FIFTH consecutive weekly report making this exact recommendation. Every day this trade is deferred while the S&P sets new records costs the portfolio measurable return. Execute at next market open, unconditionally. Post-trade: CSPX rises to approximately 31.1% of NAV; cash falls to approximately 6.9%.

Thesis strongly intact and accelerating. AWS AI revenue run rate $15B, growing 24% YoY. $200B 2026 capex commitment signals deep management conviction. CEO Jassy shareholder letter described as most impactful yet by analysts. 64 of 68 covering analysts rate Buy with $281 average price target — 20% upside from current. Stock +3.45% on April 14 alone; up 1.2% YTD. Portfolio leading performer at +16.40% unrealized. Zero dividend = 0% WHT. Hold and compound — no action required.

positions.wht_exposure: 15% Dutch WHT assumed — verify on next IBKR dividend statement
positions.rationale: Q1 2026 results (April 15): BEAT — EUR 8.8B revenue vs EUR 8.5B consensus; net income EUR 2.8B vs EUR 2.5B expected. Full-year guidance RAISED to EUR 36-40B from EUR 34-39B. Fundamental thesis fully intact: EUV monopoly, AI-driven fab capex supercycle, no credible competitor. HOWEVER: stock fell approximately 6% post-earnings due to tightening China export restrictions — one of the thesis invalidation conditions is activating. Current XML mark ($1,518.30) predates the post-earnings drop; actual value closer to ~$1,427, still above cost basis of $1,397.14. Net assessment: HOLD — strong fundamentals outweigh China restriction risk at this position size ($264). ACTION REQUIRED: verify IBKR WHT rate on next dividend statement. If 30% NRA rate confirmed, initiate switch to AMS-listed ASML to eliminate US NRA withholding entirely.

Gold at GLD $445.09, down approximately 12.5% from the January 29 all-time high of GLD $509.70. Risk-on rally (S&P at ATH, Iran ceasefire resolved) has materially reduced the immediate safe-haven bid. Structural thesis intact: central bank accumulation at multi-decade highs, USD reserve weaponisation concerns, tariff-driven inflation. At 10.30% of NAV, near the 11% target — within acceptable drift. Hold, but set an alert: if GLD breaks below $420 (a further 6% decline), trim 25% of position and redeploy into CSPX. Momentum is neutral-negative in current risk-on environment.

positions.wht_exposure: 30% on dividend — annualised drag under $1 at current size, immaterial
positions.rationale: Near breakeven at -0.02% — Liberation Day tariff shock has fully reversed. Payments duopoly thesis intact: asset-light model, pricing power, global consumer volume exposure. Position is small ($367). Designated Tranche 3 co-target: ADD $100 MA if S&P drops 5-7% from current levels, improving cost basis. No standalone add thesis at all-time high market levels — hold.

NVDA at $196.51, up +7.99% from cost $181.98. On an 11-consecutive winning session streak as of April 14 — longest on record for the stock. Hyperscaler AI capex cycle fully intact: Meta $60B, Amazon $200B 2026 commitments. ASML Q1 beat confirms semiconductor capex supercycle is robust — indirect positive for NVDA training demand. China H20 restrictions are a known, priced-in risk — the $5.5B inventory charge was taken in prior fiscal quarter. No new material China deterioration in April 2026. Strong momentum. Hold — do not chase at elevated levels.

IE-domiciled accumulating T-bill equivalent (0-3M US Treasuries) yielding approximately 4.3% with 0% effective WHT — structurally optimal for a Lebanese resident. At 13.90% of NAV, near the 15% target. Minor mark-to-market loss (-1.26%) is irrelevant for a near-zero-duration instrument. Opportunity cost is rising as S&P hits ATH and two rate cuts in 2026 will reduce yield. No immediate action — close CSPX gap first, then flag for gradual trim toward 10% of NAV as portfolio scales and rate cut cycle progresses.

rebalancing: Single priority action: BUY CSPX $250 IMMEDIATELY. This is the fifth consecutive week this recommendation has been made without execution — the deferred execution is itself now the portfolio's primary risk factor. Post-trade: CSPX rises from 25.49% to approximately 31.1% of NAV; cash falls from 12.68% to approximately 6.9%. No other rebalancing warranted — all remaining positions within acceptable drift bands. Tranche 3 (~$297.99 post-CSPX-buy): hard deadline May 3, 2026 (17 days from now). Trigger A — S&P 500 drops 5-7% from ~6,967-7,023 to ~6,480-6,500: deploy $200 CSPX + $100 MA. Trigger B (hard deadline): deploy $200 CSPX unconditionally by May 3, retain ~$97 buffer. Longer-term: as Fed cuts rates in 2026, begin gradual U03A trim from 15% toward 10% of NAV, redeploying into CSPX.
cash_strategy: $547.99 available; $497.99 deployable after $50 operating buffer. STEP 1 — IMMEDIATE: Execute overdue $250 CSPX buy. Cash falls to approximately $297.99 (~6.9% of post-trade NAV). STEP 2 — TRANCHE 3 ($297.99, window now to May 3 hard deadline, 17 days): Trigger A — S&P 500 drops 5-7% from ~6,967-7,023: deploy $200 CSPX + $100 MA. Trigger B — May 3 unconditional: deploy $200 CSPX regardless, retain ~$97.99 operating buffer. Cash above 10% of NAV is structurally return-destructive at this portfolio size and must not persist past May 3.

new_opportunities.rationale: Broadcom remains the highest-conviction new position candidate after NAV crosses $5,000. AI custom silicon leadership (Google TPU, Meta AI accelerator programs) plus networking dominance via VMware integration. Zero dividend yield = 0% WHT. Outperforming NVDA YTD. Would complement NVDA as second AI semiconductor name. DO NOT BUY NOW — close CSPX gap first; avoid fragmentation below $5K NAV. Revisit after full Tranche 3 deployment.

action_items: CRITICAL — Execute IMMEDIATELY: Buy CSPX $250 (~0.335 units at ~$747). FIFTH consecutive report. S&P 500 is at all-time highs. Deferred execution while index hits records is the portfolio's primary performance leak. No further deferral acceptable under any circumstances.

VERIFY: confirm IBKR applies 15% Dutch treaty WHT or 30% US NRA flat rate on next dividend statement. If 30% confirmed, initiate switch to AMS-listed ASML shares.
